title: hippocrepis comosa subsp. glauca
intro: Hippocrepis comosa subsp. glauca, also known as the hairy broom or blue lupine, is a plant species belonging to the Fabaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as its ability to treat respiratory issues and gastrointestinal problems. The health benefits of Hippocrepis comosa subsp. glauca include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and act as an expectorant to relieve coughs and congestion. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
